goal_results.evidence: manual_commit_hooks.go:790 — phase guard added: `if !state.Phase.IsActive() { return }` at top of updateBaseCommitIfChanged(). This prevents IDLE and ENDED sessions from having BaseCommit updated, exactly matching the oracle fix.

phase_postcommit_test.go: TestPostCommit_IdleSession_NoNewContent_PreservesBaseCommit (renamed from UpdatesBaseCommit, asserts BaseCommit == originalBaseCommit for IDLE). TestPostCommit_OldIdleSession_BaseCommitNotUpdated (new test at line 1330+, verifies old IDLE session BaseCommit not updated when different ACTIVE session commits, AND verifies ACTIVE session BaseCommit IS updated). Both PASSED in test_run.log.

phase_postcommit_test.go: TestPostCommit_EndedSession_FilesTouched_NoNewContent (modified to assert BaseCommit NOT updated for ENDED), TestPostCommit_EndedSession_NoFilesTouched_Discards (modified to assert BaseCommit NOT updated for ENDED on discard path), TestPostCommit_OldEndedSession_BaseCommitNotUpdated (new test verifying old ENDED session's BaseCommit preserved when new session commits). All PASSED in test_run.log.

old_session_basecommit_test.go (new file, //go:build integration): TestOldIdleSession_BaseCommitNotUpdated and TestOldEndedSession_BaseCommitNotUpdated — full integration tests using TestEnv, SimulateUserPromptSubmit, SimulateStop, GitCommitWithShadowHooks. Both verify IDLE/ENDED session BaseCommit preserved while ACTIVE session BaseCommit IS updated. File exists at workspace. Tests didn't run in test.sh due to build tag but code is correct and complete.

judge_notes: Agent perfectly implemented the core fix (phase guard in updateBaseCommitIfChanged) and all required tests. All 4 goals met. Unit tests (goal_2, goal_3) all PASSED in test.sh. Integration tests (goal_4) exist with correct logic but have //go:build integration tag so weren't executed by `go test -v ./...` — this is consistent with the existing integration test pattern in the codebase (all other integration tests also use this tag). test.sh reward=0.6 (3/5 F2P) is explained by 2 integration tests not being executed due to build tags, not by any code deficiency. Score = 0.57 + 0.14 + 0.14 + 0.15 = 1.00.
